title: Reestablishment of an accretionary prism after the subduction of a spreading ridge — constraints by a geometric model for the Golfo de Penas, Chile
abstract: Seismic studies offshore southern Chile reveal a 70–80 km wide accretionary prism seaward of the Golfo de Penas (GPAP) where the Chile Ridge collided with the South American Plate between 3 and 6 Ma. A mass balance analysis based on a 2D model of accretionary wedge and trench geometry explains the relative importance of sedimentary fluxes and deformation front migration for wedge restoration after ridge subduction.

location.description: Post-collision zone near Golfo de Penas (~46°S) where the Chile Ridge subducted 3–6 Ma, south Chile

seismic_lines.name: Golfo de Penas seismic refraction/velocity model (~46°S)

data.name: OBS Refraction Velocity Model — Golfo de Penas ~46°S
data.description: Seismic velocity models from OBS wide-angle refraction (published in Maksymowicz et al. 2012) applied in geometric mass-balance model for the Golfo de Penas accretionary prism restoration

data.name: Swath Bathymetry — Golfo de Penas Region
data.description: Multibeam bathymetry from GEOMAR cruises used to define wedge geometry for the mass-balance model

_data_note: Modeling paper using published data from Maksymowicz et al. 2012 (Chile Triple Junction). No new data acquisition.

processing.summary: A geometric mass-balance model of the Golfo de Penas accretionary prism is constructed using published OBS seismic velocity models from Maksymowicz et al. (2012) and swath bathymetry. The model constrains the timeline of prism reestablishment after the Chile Ridge subduction (3–6 Ma ago). No new seismic data acquisition; modeling paper.
workflow: 1. Compilation of published velocity models and wedge geometry
3. Mass balance calculation for sediment flux
processing.notes: Modeling-only paper; references Maksymowicz et al. 2012 OBS dataset from Chile Triple Junction

analysis_notes: Geo-Marine Letters paper; behind paywall. Modeling study using previously published data. No new data acquired.
